## Status of biological data for SDMs {background-image="../../shared/assets/images/panama-taxonomic-workshop-2025.jpg" background-size="cover" background-position="center" .section-photo}

<div class="section-title-panel">
<h2>Status of biological data for SDMs</h2>
<p>Compiled datasets, data gaps, and readiness for modeling</p>
</div>

---

## Status of biological data for SDMs {background-color="white" .technical-white}

<div class="sb5-header">IKI - Salvando a los Cinco Azules (SB5)</div>
<h2 class="slide-title">Status of biological data for SDMs</h2>

The biological data workflow preserves, harmonizes, quality-controls, and exports telemetry and observation records before SDM modeling.

- Raw data are kept auditable and unchanged
- Telemetry and observations are standardized separately
- SDM-ready files preserve provenance and QC decisions
- Final model inputs keep marine SDM-compatible coordinates
- Viewer: [Megafauna data viewer Shiny app](https://isaakbm-megafauna-data-viewer.share.connect.posit.cloud/)

<div class="repo-note">Local reference: shared/references/repos/pre-sdm-bio-data-prep-readme.md</div>

<div class="sb5-footer">isaaksonbm · ibrito@conservation.org</div>

---

## Megafauna data viewer {background-color="white" .technical-white .app-demo}

<div class="sb5-header">IKI - Salvando a los Cinco Azules (SB5)</div>
<h2 class="slide-title">Megafauna data viewer</h2>

<div class="app-grid">
<div>

![](../../shared/assets/screenshots/megafauna-data-viewer.png){.screenshot}

</div>
<div>

Use the viewer to inspect:

- species-level records
- spatial coverage
- temporal coverage
- data sources
- modeling readiness

[Open app](https://isaakbm-megafauna-data-viewer.share.connect.posit.cloud/)

![](../../shared/assets/qr/megafauna-data-viewer-qr.png){.qr}

</div>
</div>

<div class="sb5-footer">isaaksonbm · ibrito@conservation.org</div>

---

## Data readiness is not uniform {background-color="white" .technical-white}

<div class="sb5-header">IKI - Salvando a los Cinco Azules (SB5)</div>
<h2 class="slide-title">Data readiness is not uniform</h2>

The goal is to make data strengths and limitations visible before modeling decisions are made.

- Stronger data support more confident model interpretation
- Sparse data require simpler or more cautious interpretation
- Biased sampling can shape apparent habitat relationships
- Expert feedback is needed to prioritize realistic improvements

<div class="metric-grid">
<div><strong>42,475</strong> filtered records in viewer</div>
<div><strong>8</strong> SB5 species in current app view</div>
<div><strong>71</strong> data sources</div>
<div><strong>2</strong> data types: telemetry and observations</div>
</div>

<div class="sb5-footer">isaaksonbm · ibrito@conservation.org</div>
